errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
errorBoundary.persistHint: If the problem persists, try switching to a different tab or restarting the application.

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.caption: Application keyboard shortcuts
shortcuts.closeSearch: Close search / close DM panel (Chat tab)
shortcuts.footerNote: Cmd/Ctrl+0, A, M, and S switch to the App, Diagnostics, Stats, and Packet Sniffer tabs by name when those tabs are visible (not by fixed slot, so shortcuts stay correct when some tabs are hidden).

logPanel.allFiltersOff: All level filters are off. Enable at least one under Show levels.
logPanel.noAppLinesMatch: No app lines match the current filters.
logPanel.noDeviceLinesMatch: No device lines match the current filters.
logPanel.writtenToFile: All levels are still written to the log file; filters only affect this panel.

app.meshtasticQueueTooltip: Transmit queue: packets waiting to be sent. Green = low, amber = filling up, red = congested.
app.meshcoreQueueTooltip: Because MeshCore sends messages rapidly, and we poll every 30 seconds, this should always be 0. If not 0, there is congestion.

radioPanel.configImportedNoChanges: Config imported. No device changes to apply.
radioPanel.configImportedPartialApplied: Config imported. Applied to device: {{applied}}. Not supported by this device: {{notSupported}}.
radioPanel.configImportedPartialNoApplied: Config imported. Not supported by this device: {{notSupported}}.
radioPanel.invalidCoordinates: Invalid coordinates — enter valid lat/lon values.
radioPanel.meshcoreGpsFailed: Device GPS set failed ({{message}}). Using App Location (static/browser/IP) for map position.
radioPanel.title: Radio Configuration
radioPanel.ntpHint: Leave empty for default NTP server.
radioPanel.restartWarning: The device may briefly restart after applying new LoRa or device settings.
radioPanel.flashMemoryNote: Changes are written to the device's flash memory and persist across reboots.
radioPanel.setKeyFromSha256: Set key from SHA-256(name) first 16 bytes (MeshCore #channels)
radioPanel.removeAllContactsTitle: Remove all contacts from radio, keep in database
radioPanel.keySizeTooltip: None = no encryption. Simple = default Meshtastic key (shared by all default-config devices — not private). AES-128/256 = custom private key. All nodes on this channel must use the same key.
radioPanel.encryptionKeyTooltip: Base64-encoded encryption key. AES-128 = 16 bytes (24 base64 chars). AES-256 = 32 bytes (44 base64 chars). Use 'Generate' for a random key, or paste a shared key from another node.
radioPanel.connectToConfigure: Connect to a device to modify configuration.
radioPanel.deviceMetricsDescription: Mesh broadcast: how often battery, voltage, and channel utilization are sent over LoRa. 0 = disabled; default 1800 s (30 min). Connected app: when linked over Bluetooth, serial, or Wi-Fi, the firmware often still pushes device metrics to this client about every 60 s for on-screen freshness—that is not the same as your mesh interval.
radioPanel.deviceMetricsTooltip: Meshtastic firmware sends device metrics to a connected client roughly once per minute for local monitoring, regardless of the mesh telemetry interval. Official reference: https://meshtastic.org/docs/configuration/module/telemetry
radioPanel.bluetoothToggleDesc: Toggle Bluetooth radio on the device.
radioPanel.pairingPinDesc: 6-digit fixed PIN for Bluetooth pairing. Default: 123456.
radioPanel.meshcoreSha256KeyTitle: Set key from SHA-256(name) first 16 bytes (MeshCore #channels)
radioPanel.securityOpenLocationUplinkTooltip: Unencrypted location sent to internet via MQTT
radioPanel.validationAes128: AES-128 key must be exactly 16 bytes (24 base64 chars)
radioPanel.validationAes256: AES-256 key must be exactly 32 bytes (44 base64 chars)
radioPanel.dangerZonePermanent: These actions are permanent and cannot be undone.
radioPanel.resetNodeDbMessage: This will clear the device's internal node database. The device will re-discover nodes over time.
radioPanel.shutdownMessage: This will power off the connected device. You will need to physically power it back on.
radioPanel.factoryResetConfigMessage: This will reset device configuration to factory defaults, but preserves your node database. Settings will need to be reconfigured.
radioPanel.factoryResetMessage: This will erase ALL device settings and restore factory defaults. All channels, configuration, and stored data on the device will be permanently lost. This action CANNOT be undone.

securityPanel.keyRegenRequested: Key regeneration requested. Device will generate new keys.
securityPanel.keysBackedUp: Keys backed up to system keychain.
securityPanel.privateKeyImported: Private key imported. Restart may be required.
securityPanel.connectToManage: Connect to a device to manage security settings.
securityPanel.privateKeyHint: Keep your private key secret. It is used to encrypt direct messages.
securityPanel.adminKeysIntro: Up to {{max}} public keys authorized to send admin commands to this device. Each must be a base64-encoded 32-byte Curve25519 public key.
securityPanel.invalidAdminKey: Must be a valid base64-encoded 32-byte key
securityPanel.managedDeviceDesc: Device is managed by a mesh administrator.
securityPanel.serialConsoleDesc: Enable serial console over the Stream API.
securityPanel.debugLogApiDesc: Output live debug logging over serial or Bluetooth.
securityPanel.adminChannelDesc: Allow incoming device control over the insecure legacy admin channel.
securityPanel.keyBackupUnavailable: System keychain encryption is not available on this platform. Backup and restore are disabled.
securityPanel.keyBackupDesc: Back up your DM keys to the system keychain (encrypted). You can restore them to the device at any time.
securityPanel.meshcoreCryptoDesc: Sign data with your device key, or export/import your private key for backup.
securityPanel.exportKeyWarning: Keep this key secure. Anyone with access can decrypt your private messages.
securityPanel.regenerateKeysConfirm: Regenerating keys will replace your current DM public and private keys. Any existing encrypted messages will no longer be decryptable. Are you sure?

modulePanel.title: Module Configuration
modulePanel.connectToDevice: Connect to a device to modify module configuration.
modulePanel.waitingForModuleConfig: Waiting for module config from device…
modulePanel.telemetryDeviceMetricsDescription: Mesh broadcast: how often battery, voltage, and channel utilization are sent over LoRa via the telemetry module. 0 = disabled. Connected app: when linked, device metrics to this client often refresh about every 60 s—separate from the mesh/module interval.
modulePanel.telemetryDeviceMetricsTooltip: Meshtastic firmware sends device metrics to a connected client roughly once per minute for local monitoring, regardless of the mesh telemetry interval. Official reference: https://meshtastic.org/docs/configuration/module/telemetry

fields.ledEnabledDesc: Turn the onboard RGB LED on or off.
fields.detectionSensorDesc: Broadcast detection events (PIR, door sensor, etc.) to the mesh.
fields.minBroadcastIntervalDesc: Minimum seconds between broadcasts even if repeatedly triggered.
fields.stateBroadcastIntervalDesc: How often to broadcast the current state even without a change.
fields.extNotifModuleDesc: Enable GPIO-based external alerts for buzzers, vibration motors, and LEDs.
fields.activeHighDesc: GPIO active level. On = active high (3.3 V triggers output); Off = active low.
fields.primaryOutputGpioDesc: GPIO pin for primary notification output. 0 = unset.
fields.vibrationGpioDesc: GPIO pin for vibration motor. 0 = unset.
fields.outputDurationDesc: How long to keep the output active per alert (milliseconds).
fields.nagTimeoutDesc: Keep repeating the alert for this many seconds. 0 = single trigger only.
fields.alertOnMessageDesc: Trigger primary output GPIO when a text message is received.
fields.buzzerOnMessageDesc: Also trigger buzzer GPIO on incoming messages.
fields.vibrationOnMessageDesc: Also trigger vibration GPIO on incoming messages.
fields.alertOnBellDesc: Trigger primary output GPIO on bell / tapback signals.
fields.buzzerOnBellDesc: Also trigger buzzer GPIO on bell signals.
fields.vibrationOnBellDesc: Also trigger vibration GPIO on bell signals.
fields.usePwmBuzzerDesc: Use PWM frequency for tone-capable buzzers (e.g. RAK buzzer).
fields.useI2sBuzzerDesc: Use I2S audio output as buzzer (T-Watch S3, T-Deck with speaker).
fields.mqttRelayEnabledDesc: Enable the device to relay packets to/from an MQTT broker.
fields.serverAddressDesc: Leave empty to use default mqtt.meshtastic.org
fields.rootTopicDesc: Topic prefix (e.g. 'msh/US'). Leave empty for default.
fields.encryptionEnabledDesc: Encrypt packets before publishing to MQTT.
fields.jsonOutputEnabledDesc: Also publish decoded JSON payloads alongside protobuf.
fields.mapReportingEnabledDesc: Periodically publish node position to the Meshtastic map.
fields.paxCounterEnabledDesc: Count nearby Bluetooth and WiFi devices as a crowd density metric.
fields.paxUpdateIntervalDesc: How often to broadcast pax counter readings. 0 = use default.
fields.senderIntervalDesc: How often this node sends range test packets. 0 = receiver only.
fields.saveResultsToFileDesc: Log range test results to the device filesystem.
fields.sfHeartbeatDesc: Periodically broadcast presence so clients know S&F is available.
fields.sfHistoryMaxDesc: Max messages to return when a node requests history.
fields.sfHistoryWindowDesc: How far back in time to return messages (seconds).
fields.telEnvIntervalDesc: How often to send temperature/humidity/pressure. 0 = disabled.
fields.messagesOnePerLine: Messages (one per line, max 30 chars each)
fields.cannedMessagesPlaceholder:
Hello
OK
On my way
Need help
fields.cannedMessagesHint: Enter one message per line. Used with the input peripheral buttons.
fields.serialModuleEnabledDesc: Enable serial port data forwarding via the mesh.
fields.serialEchoDesc: Echo received serial data back to sender.
fields.sfEnabledDesc: Buffer messages for nodes that rejoin the mesh.
fields.sfNumRecordsDesc: Maximum messages to store. 0 = use default.
fields.ipTunnelHint: IP Tunnel packets forward network traffic through the mesh. Configure tunnel settings in the device network configuration.
fields.historyReturnMaxDesc: Max messages to return when a node requests history.
fields.historyReturnWindowDesc: How far back in time to return messages (seconds).
fields.remoteHardwareHint: GPIO messages are received automatically when enabled on the device. Configure GPIO pins in the device hardware settings.

appPanel.failedSaveRetention: Failed to save message retention setting.
appPanel.invalidLatitude: Invalid latitude. Must be between -90 and 90.
appPanel.invalidLongitude: Invalid longitude. Must be between -180 and 180.
appPanel.noNoFixNodes: No no-fix or zero-island nodes found.
appPanel.noGpsPosition: No GPS position available. Use device node coordinates or enable GPS in the app.
appPanel.noNodesAboveDistance: No nodes found beyond the distance threshold.
appPanel.deletedContactsNoPubkey: Deleted {{deleted}} contacts. {{excludedStubCount}} chat stub nodes excluded.
appPanel.filterDistantNodes: Filter distant nodes from map and node list
appPanel.hideMqttOnlyNodes: Hide MQTT-only nodes from map and node list
appPanel.autoPruneNodesOlderThan: Auto-prune nodes on startup, older than
appPanel.autoPrunePositionHistory: Auto-prune position history on startup, older than
appPanel.removeContactsNeverAdvertised: Remove contacts that have never advertised on startup
appPanel.autoPruneUnheardContacts: Auto-prune unheard contacts on startup, older than
appPanel.capTotalContacts: Cap total contacts, keep most recently seen
appPanel.pruneNoFixNodes: Prune No-Fix / Zero Island Nodes Removes nodes with null or near-zero coordinates (no GPS fix or at 0 deg N, 0 deg E).
appPanel.pruneDistantNodes: Prune Distant Nodes Beyond the distance threshold in Map & Node Filtering. Requires a valid GPS location.
appPanel.pruneOfflineNodes: Prune Offline Nodes that have not been heard within the offline threshold
appPanel.title: App Settings
appPanel.floodAdvertScheduleLabel: Automatically send a flood advert on a schedule:
appPanel.staticPositionDesc: Set a precise static position. When saved, this overrides browser and IP-based location.
appPanel.autoRefreshDisabledStatic: Auto-refresh is disabled while a static position is active.
appPanel.mapFilterDesc: Hides nodes beyond a set distance from your device. Filtering is display-only — nodes remain in the database.
appPanel.noGpsFix: Your device has no GPS fix — filter is enabled but all nodes are shown.
appPanel.requiresGpsFix: Note: Requires your device to have a valid GPS fix.
appPanel.dataManagementDesc: Export your local database (messages & nodes) as a .db file, or import/merge another user's database into yours.
appPanel.dangerZoneDesc: These actions are permanent and cannot be undone. Confirm each step carefully.
appPanel.pruneDistantNodesDesc: Beyond the distance threshold in Map & Node Filtering. Requires a valid GPS location.
appPanel.pruneOfflineNodesDays: Not heard in over {{days}} days. Favorited nodes are excluded.
appPanel.deleteContactsNoPubkeysDesc: Excludes chat stub nodes created from messages.

nodeListPanel.snrTooltip: SNR in dB — only meaningful for direct (0-hop) RF neighbors
nodeListPanel.echoesTooltip: Echoes: same packet received via multiple paths (e.g. RF + MQTT or multiple RF hops). Higher means better mesh redundancy.
nodeListPanel.signalBarsTooltip: Signal bars (RSSI) only for direct (0-hop) RF neighbors

meshcoreContactSettings.overwriteOldest: Overwrite oldest non-favourite contacts when full
meshcoreContactSettings.showRefreshControl: Show refresh control on contacts list
meshcoreContactSettings.showPublicKeys: Show public keys in contacts list

connectionPanel.stayOnTab: For best results, stay on this tab until the device has finished connecting.
connectionPanel.adaDfuHint: On macOS, if a device shows as "AdaDFU", pair it first in System Settings → Bluetooth to see its Meshtastic name.
connectionPanel.meshcorePairingNote: Pair your MeshCore device in system Bluetooth settings before connecting. Use a PIN code if prompted — if your system does not ask for a PIN, the connection will fail and you may need to remove the pairing and re-pair with a PIN.
connectionPanel.noSerialPorts: No serial ports found. Ensure your device is plugged in.
connectionPanel.enterPin: Enter the PIN shown on your device:
connectionPanel.pinExpiring: {{count}}s — enter PIN quickly, BlueZ pairing window is closing
connectionPanel.manualContactApprovalDesc: Require manual approval before new contacts appear
connectionPanel.deviceAddressHint: Enter hostname or IP address (without http://)
connectionPanel.windowsMdnsNote: On Windows, meshtastic.local may not resolve — use the device's IP address instead.
connectionPanel.meshcoreHostHint: MeshCore companion radio host (connects on port 5000)
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticBle1: Ensure your Meshtastic device has Bluetooth enabled and is in range.
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticBle2: Click Connect to scan — a device picker will appear with discovered Meshtastic devices.
connectionPanel.hintMeshcoreBle1: Ensure your MeshCore device has Bluetooth enabled and is in range.
connectionPanel.hintMeshcoreBle2: Click Connect to scan for nearby MeshCore devices.
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticSerial1: Connect your Meshtastic device via USB cable.
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticSerial2: Click Connect — a port picker will appear with available serial ports.
connectionPanel.hintMeshcoreSerial1: Connect your MeshCore device via USB cable.
connectionPanel.hintMeshcoreSerial2: Click Connect — a port picker will appear with available serial ports.
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticHttp1: Enter the IP address or hostname of a WiFi-connected Meshtastic node. The device must have WiFi enabled in its config.
connectionPanel.hintMeshtasticHttp2: If connection is unreliable (e.g. with meshtastic.local), try the device's IP address instead — desktop mDNS can be flaky.
connectionPanel.hintMeshcoreHttp: Enter the hostname or IP address of your MeshCore companion radio. It must be reachable on port 5000.
connectionPanel.stageLinuxUnpaired: Saved device is not paired in Linux Bluetooth — select it below to enter PIN and connect.
connectionPanel.stageScanning: Scanning — select your device when it appears below
connectionPanel.stageEnterPin: Enter the PIN shown on your device
connectionPanel.stagePairingFailed: Pairing failed. Please re-pair your device.
connectionPanel.stageEnterPinLinux: Enter the PIN shown on your device (pair with Linux first)
connectionPanel.liamServerNote: Liam's server is uplink-only — your node appears on his map but you won't receive messages from it.

serial.accessDeniedWindowsHint: Ensure the correct USB driver is installed (CH340, CP210x, or FTDI). Check Device Manager for a yellow warning on the COM port.
serial.accessDeniedLinuxHint: On Linux, add your user to the dialout group: sudo usermod -aG dialout $USER (then log out and back in)
serial.disconnectedHint: Ensure the USB cable is connected and the device is powered on.
serial.timeoutHint: If the port appeared briefly, try reconnecting the USB cable.

http.timeoutMdnsWindows: On Windows, meshtastic.local requires Bonjour (installed with iTunes). Use the device's IP address instead.
http.timeoutMdnsNonWindows: mDNS may not resolve — try the device's IP address instead.
http.timeoutGeneric: Ensure the device is powered on and reachable.
http.econnrefusedHint: Ensure the device is powered on and connected to the same network.
http.suffixMdnsWindows: On Windows, meshtastic.local requires Bonjour (installed with iTunes). Use the device's IP address instead.
http.suffixMdnsNonWindows: mDNS may not resolve on your network; try the device's IP address instead.

ble.adapterWindowsHint: Check Settings > Bluetooth & devices. If Bluetooth is on but unavailable, update your Bluetooth driver in Device Manager.
ble.adapterLinuxHint: Make sure Bluetooth is enabled and the Web Bluetooth experimental flag is set. Try: systemctl status bluetooth
ble.adapterGenericHint: Make sure Bluetooth is enabled. On Linux, run: systemctl status bluetooth
ble.securityPermissionHint: Bluetooth permission denied. Ensure the app has access to the Bluetooth device.
ble.gattDisconnectedHint: GATT connection dropped. Try moving closer to the device and reconnecting.
ble.gattNotSupportedBase: The device requires pairing before connecting. Use the "Remove & Re-pair Device" button to re-initiate pairing.
ble.gattNotSupportedLinuxPin: For Meshtastic use PIN 123456. For MeshCore the PIN is shown on the device display.
ble.authFailedBase: Bluetooth authentication failed ({{message}}). The device may not be properly paired. Use the "Remove & Re-pair Device" button.
ble.networkFailedBase: Bluetooth connection failed ({{message}}). The device may not be properly paired.
ble.networkFailedLinuxHint: Use the "Remove & Re-pair Device" button or manage pairings via 'bluetoothctl'.
ble.networkFailedNonLinuxHint: Remove the device from Bluetooth settings and re-pair.
ble.connectionAttemptFailedBase: The device may not be paired with your computer. Remove the device from Bluetooth settings, then re-pair it. For Meshtastic use PIN 123456. For MeshCore the PIN is randomly generated and displayed on the device.
ble.connectionAttemptFailedLinuxHint: On Linux, you can manage pairings via the system Bluetooth settings or 'bluetoothctl' tool.
ble.meshcoreHandshakeHint: Ensure your MeshCore device is in Bluetooth Companion mode and paired with your computer using a PIN. Remove any existing pairing and re-pair if connection issues persist.
ble.meshcoreHandshakeWindowsExtra: On Windows, toggle Bluetooth off/on and update the adapter driver in Device Manager if disconnects persist.

error.noMacRePair: No device MAC address available for re-pairing
error.pinFormat: PIN must be 1–6 digits (use the code shown on the device).
error.pinAcceptedNext: PIN accepted. Pairing completed. Press Connect and select your device to finish connecting.

diagnosticsPanel.dropRoutingRows: Drop routing rows older than {{hours}} hours (1–168)
diagnosticsPanel.meshcoreInterferenceDescription: These entries identify on-frequency MeshCore interference heard by this radio. They can contribute to utilization and retry issues, but are not Meshtastic route-failure diagnostics by themselves.
diagnosticsPanel.excludeMqttData: Exclude this node's MQTT data from diagnostics
diagnosticsPanel.stopIgnoringMqtt: Click to stop ignoring MQTT for this node
diagnosticsPanel.scopeSubtitle: Observed at this client — path mix is from packets heard at this connected node.
diagnosticsPanel.ipGeolocationBanner: Using city-level IP geolocation — distance-based thresholds are doubled to reduce false positives. For accurate routing analysis, connect a device with GPS or set a static position.

rawPacketLog.hashColumnTooltip: First four bytes after path prefix; not a node id. Meaning depends on payload type.
rawPacketLog.filterPlaceholder: Filter by type, name, or hex...
rawPacketLog.emptyWaiting: No new mesh packets received yet. Please wait...
rawPacketLog.noPacketsMatchFilter: No packets match the current filter.

repeatersPanel.noRepeatersHintPre: Repeaters appear when contacts with type "Repeater" advertise. Use
repeatersPanel.snrDbTooltip: dB — from Request Status when available, else contact list
repeatersPanel.rssiDbmTooltip: dBm — from Request Status when available, else contact list
repeatersPanel.hopCountTooltip: Hop count from last Ping trace (repeaters between you and this node)
repeatersPanel.cayenneLppTooltip: Cayenne LPP sensor payload (not advert GPS on the map)
repeatersPanel.noLppData: No LPP sensor data in this response.
repeatersPanel.noTelemetryResponse: No telemetry response yet. Try Sensor (LPP) again.
repeatersPanel.mapPositionFromAdvert: Map position comes from advert/contact data, not this sensor request.
repeatersPanel.mapPositionFromTelemetry: Map position comes from advert/contact data, not sensor telemetry.

nodeDetailModal.nodeIncomplete: Node data incomplete - waiting for full NodeInfo packet
nodeDetailModal.hasPublicKey: Has public key - can send DMs
nodeDetailModal.dbOnlyContact: Contact stored in database only, not on radio
nodeDetailModal.syncedContact: Contact synced: stored in database and on radio
nodeDetailModal.radioOnlyContact: Contact on radio but not yet fully stored in database
nodeDetailModal.cayenneLppTitle: Cayenne LPP sensor payload (not advert GPS on the map)

logAnalyzeModal.title: Log Analysis
logAnalyzeModal.protocolNote: Uses the active radio protocol for protocol-specific categories; individual log lines are not labeled by protocol.
logAnalyzeModal.emptyState: No issues detected in the current log view.

packetDistribution.noSenderId: No sender id in raw frame

meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.description: Controls how your node responds to telemetry requests and what data is included (MeshCore companion firmware). Per-contact permissions apply when you choose "specific contacts"; edit contact flags from the reference MeshCore apps or a future contact detail UI.
meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.allowAllBase: Telemetry requests will be allowed from everyone.
meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.allowAllLoc: Location will be included in your telemetry.
meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.denyLoc: Location will be excluded from your telemetry.
meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.allowAllEnv: Environment sensors will be included in your telemetry.
meshcoreTelemetryPrivacy.denyEnv: Environment sensors will be excluded from your telemetry.

takServerPanel.title: TAK Server
takServerPanel.description: Built-in TAK server for ATAK / WinTAK / iTAK compatibility. Broadcasts mesh node positions as Cursor on Target (CoT) events.
takServerPanel.atakPluginDesc: ATAK plugin packets received from mesh nodes with ATAK plugin enabled.
takServerPanel.noAtakMessages: No ATAK plugin messages received from mesh.
takServerPanel.atakDataPackageDesc: Generate a .zip file containing TLS certificates and connection settings for import into ATAK, WinTAK, or iTAK.
takServerPanel.certificatesDesc: Self-signed CA, server, and client certificates are generated automatically on first start and stored in the app data directory.
takServerPanel.regenerateWarning: Regenerating invalidates existing ATAK data packages — you will need to re-import.

nodeInfoBody.connectionHealthTooltip: Based on same packet received via multiple paths (e.g. RF + MQTT or multiple RF receptions).
nodeInfoBody.noNodeTelemetry: No node telemetry. Node diagnostics unavailable.
nodeInfoBody.loraCollisionNote: To detect MeshCore specifically, the device must log decode failures with the packet's first byte (0x3c). Until then only the generic collision message is available.
nodeInfoBody.offenseHeuristic: Route efficiency unclear — many hops with strong signal (heuristic only)
nodeInfoBody.offenseOverhopping: Node is over-hopping for its distance or signal strength
nodeInfoBody.offenseRoutingLoop: Possible routing loop — high packet duplication detected
nodeInfoBody.offenseRouteFlapping: Route is unstable — hop count changing frequently
nodeInfoBody.offenseZeroHops: Reported as 0 hops but GPS data suggests otherwise

ble-connection.label: BLE Connection Issues
ble-connection.recommendation: BLE connection unstable. Check distance to device and Bluetooth adapter status.

mqtt.label: MQTT Issues
mqtt.recommendation: MQTT connection issues. Verify broker URL, credentials, and network connectivity.

mqtt-retries-exhausted.label: MQTT Reconnect Limit
mqtt-retries-exhausted.recommendation: MQTT gave up after max reconnects. Confirm the broker is reachable, credentials and port are correct, and firewalls allow outbound traffic.

watchdog.label: Watchdog Triggers
watchdog.recommendation: Watchdog triggered reconnection. Device communication may be unstable.

handshake.label: Handshake Failures
handshake.recommendation: Connection handshake failed. Try reconnecting manually.

ble-connect-race.label: BLE Connect Race/Timeout
ble-connect-race.recommendation: BLE handshake timed out or raced with disconnect. Check BLE connection stability and distance to device.

auth-decrypt.label: Auth/Decryption Failures
auth-decrypt.recommendation: Authentication or decryption failure. Verify channel keys match between devices.

native-module.label: Native Module Load Failure
native-module.recommendation: A native add-on failed to load — often wrong Electron ABI after an upgrade. Run pnpm install in the project folder (or pnpm run rebuild), quit all app instances, and retry.

internal-error.label: Internal App Errors
internal-error.recommendation: An unexpected internal error occurred. Please report this bug with the log file.

database-error.label: Database Failures
database-error.recommendation: Database error. This may indicate a corrupt database file or permission issue.

database-chmod.label: Database chmod (non-fatal)
database-chmod.recommendation: Database file permission tweak failed (often benign on Windows). If you see real DB errors elsewhere, check antivirus and folder permissions for the mesh-client userData directory.

database-writable.label: Database Not Writable
database-writable.recommendation: The app cannot write its database folder. Fix permissions on the mesh-client userData directory (see troubleshooting: Database directory is not writable).

tak-server.label: TAK Server Issues
tak-server.recommendation: TAK server error. Check certificate status and port availability.

updater.label: App Updater Errors
updater.recommendation: App updater error. Check your internet connection or try a manual update from GitHub.

meshcore-tcp.label: MeshCore IP Bridge Errors
meshcore-tcp.recommendation: MeshCore TCP bridge error. Verify the remote host and port are reachable.

ble-meshcore-notify-watchdog.label: BLE Notify Watchdog (MeshCore)
ble-meshcore-notify-watchdog.recommendation: No GATT notify data within the watchdog window (common on Windows). Retry connect; ensure the radio is paired in OS Bluetooth settings before connecting.

bluetooth-pairing.label: Bluetooth Pairing Issues
bluetooth-pairing.recommendation: Bluetooth pairing or unpair failed, or the PIN prompt timed out. Open the app when pairing, enter the PIN promptly, check the MAC address and Bluetooth permissions, or remove and re-pair from OS Bluetooth settings.

sdk-meshtastic.label: Meshtastic SDK Warnings/Errors
sdk-meshtastic.recommendation: Meshtastic stack reported a warning or error. If it repeats, check firmware, transport (BLE/serial), and reconnect.

sdk-meshcore.label: MeshCore SDK Warnings/Errors
sdk-meshcore.recommendation: MeshCore stack reported a warning or error. If it repeats, check BLE pairing, MQTT settings, and reconnect.

meshCongestion.mostDuplicateProneTitle: Most RF duplicate-prone traffic lately (by originator — not which relay)
meshCongestion.partialSamples: So far, {{count}} duplicate packet(s) had multiple paths; {{pct}}% involved MQTT — need a few more samples to confirm the main cause. If you use a gateway with MQTT downlink, try Ignore MQTT for that node in Diagnostics to see if duplicates drop.
meshCongestion.insufficientEvidence: Duplicates are likely from overlapping RF coverage, MQTT+RF double delivery if you use a gateway, or repeaters with a high hop limit — we have not logged enough multi-path packets yet to say which dominates.
meshCongestion.routingAnomalies: This mesh currently has routing anomalies (long routes close in) — that increases duplicate receptions until routes improve.
meshCongestion.pathMixMqttCausal: Path mix: {{pct}}% of recent multi-path packets involved MQTT — check gateway downlink overlap or Ignore MQTT for bridged nodes.
meshCongestion.pathMixMqttHeavy: Path mix: {{pct}}% of recent multi-path packets often involve MQTT — overlapping RF+MQTT may duplicate deliveries.
meshCongestion.pathMixRfOnly: Path mix: {{pct}}% of recent multi-path packets are RF-only (no MQTT on those paths) — overlapping RF coverage or repeaters with a high hop limit are the likely cause; reducing hop limit on strong local nodes usually helps.
